title: Todd Packer

jokes.text: Clean up on aisle five.
jokes.setup: Standard retail/grocery store announcement phrase
jokes.punchline: Sets up callback for later action sequence
jokes.explanation: Simple setup that pays off later - efficient foreshadowing disguised as throwaway line

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Attention shoppers, we have a cleanup on aisle 5.

jokes.text: After three years of writing, one year of shooting, four years of re-shooting, and two years of editing
jokes.setup: Normal timeline for making a movie
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously extended timeline revealing obsessive perfectionism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott delusion - the escalating timeline reveals his complete lack of perspective

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, here's the timeline for our movie. We'll shoot for two weeks, edit for one week, have it ready in three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? Let's make it perfect. Four weeks of shooting, two weeks of editing.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm thinking about the color grading. Six weeks of shooting, four weeks of editing, two weeks of color correction.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And the sound design — we need pristine audio. Eight weeks shooting, six weeks editing, three weeks color, four weeks sound design.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, Scorsese took a year on the last one. Let's do eighteen months. Actually, two years. This is going to be a masterpiece.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We're looking at three years minimum. Maybe four. We need it to be perfect.

jokes.text: Did you guys know that our own Michael Scott has made a movie, and that he maybe will let us watch it, but only if everybody's dying to see it?
jokes.setup: Erin's earnest announcement style
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals Michael's manipulative reverse psychology
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocent directness exposes Michael's transparent manipulation

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: You guys, Michael told me that if I didn't want to stay late, I should definitely stay late, because that's what quitters do.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: So I'm staying late! Wait... why would he say that if he didn't want me to stay late?

jokes.text: Yeah, if Michael Scott did your home movie.
jokes.setup: Comparison to a home movie
jokes.punchline: Implies Michael's involvement makes it much worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim deadpan - economical put-down that's mean but accurate

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's like a home movie, except Michael directed it.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So it's terrible.

jokes.text: We thought it was a comedy. Everything pointed to it being a comedy.
jokes.setup: Previous screening went badly
jokes.punchline: They laughed at what was meant to be serious
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central comedy premise - unintentionally funny serious movie

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: They laughed at what was meant to be serious.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's not great.

jokes.text: Thanks, Mom.
jokes.setup: Pam giving earnest pep talk about being positive
jokes.punchline: Ryan dismissively calls her Mom
jokes.explanation: Basic snark - efficient but predictable put-down

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Thanks, Mom.

jokes.text: I wanted Samuel's voice to be like this. But Michael thought that Samuel should be a very advanced android, almost indistinguishable from a real person.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his robot character voice choice
jokes.punchline: Michael's 'direction' results in Dwight just talking normally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight earnestness meeting Michael's terrible direction - absurd logic delivered seriously

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I chose a robot voice because it's efficient and removes all human emotion from the equation.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. Do it more... natural. Like you're just talking to me.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Okay. So, I'm efficient and I remove all human emotion from the equation.

jokes.text: Dwight does not play a robot.
jokes.setup: Dwight's elaborate explanation of his robot character
jokes.punchline: Jim's flat contradiction of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim deadpan timing - maximum comedy from minimal words

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: My robot character has seventeen different combat modes, a plasma cannon built into his left arm, and can travel at speeds up to 200 miles per hour.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's not a robot. That's just you.

jokes.text: I gave up a lot of weekends, because I thought it would be good for my daughter to see a black man as President, even in a silly home movie. What a stupid waste of time.
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining his noble motivation for participating
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret and harsh assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Stanley - starts with surprising depth then pivots to brutal honesty

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: I'm doing this for the children.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: What the hell was I thinking?

jokes.text: Heads I do it, tails I don't. Best out of seven.
jokes.setup: Standard coin flip decision
jokes.punchline: Arbitrarily changes to best of seven to get desired result
jokes.explanation: Good character logic - Michael writing himself to always win through rule changes

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Okay, we'll flip a coin. Heads, we do it my way. Tails, we do it your way.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Tails. Okay, best two out of three.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Best two out of three goes to me. Best three out of five.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Best three out of five, also me. Best four out of seven.

jokes.text: Looks like there's gonna be a clean up on aisle five.
jokes.setup: Earlier throwaway line about cleanup on aisle five
jokes.punchline: Used as action movie one-liner before violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - transforms mundane phrase into ridiculous action hero quip

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Cleanup on aisle five.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: *shoots gun* ...There's gonna be cleanup on aisle five.

jokes.text: See, I'm gonna lure him here, then I'm gonna kill everybody, then I'm gonna dig up Scarn's dead wife, and I'm gonna hump her real good.
jokes.setup: Standard villain monologue
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurdly crude necrophilia threat
jokes.explanation: Shock value humor that lands through sheer audacity, though relies on crude escalation

jokes.text: I did not love the dialog, or the character. I took the role to impress a receptionist, who will remain nameless.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his motivation for being in the movie
jokes.punchline: Admits it was to impress Pam (the receptionist)
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment - Jim's real motivation revealed through movie commentary

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I mean, yeah, okay, fine. I did it to impress Pam.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: She's the receptionist, and... I don't know, I thought it would be cool.

jokes.text: I'm not here to learn how to mop. I'm here to learn how to play hockey. Mop it.
jokes.setup: Training montage trope with wise mentor
jokes.punchline: Training consists entirely of mopping
jokes.explanation: Good parody of training montage clichés - subverts expectations with mundane activity

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Alright Cherokee Jack, I'm ready to learn from the master.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Good. Your training begins now.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: You see this mop? This is everything.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I don't understand. When do we learn the martial arts? The weapons training?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: The mop IS the martial art. The mop IS the weapon.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So... we're just mopping?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: We're just mopping.

jokes.text: Nice try, Goldenface. Except you forgot one thing, to kill me. Oh, I wasn't trying to kill you. I was trying to slow you down.
jokes.setup: Hero survives and taunts villain with classic line
jokes.punchline: Villain reveals he wasn't actually trying to kill him
jokes.explanation: Smart subversion of action movie tropes - villain's competent response deflates hero moment

dialogue.speaker: Michael as Scarn
dialogue.line: You'll never get away with this, Goldenface!

dialogue.speaker: Jim as Goldenface
dialogue.line: Oh, but I already have. And by the way, I was never actually trying to kill you.

dialogue.speaker: Michael as Scarn
dialogue.line: What? Then what were you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Jim as Goldenface
dialogue.line: Trying to make you care about me. It's the only way I knew how.

jokes.text: How's your wife doing?
jokes.setup: Casual question about wife's wellbeing
jokes.punchline: Villain asking about the wife he murdered
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy through casual delivery of cruel taunt - economy of language

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: How's your wife?

dialogue.speaker: Goldenface
dialogue.line: She's dead. I killed her.

jokes.text: I am so sorry I have to do this. I'm so sorry. I'm so sorry.
jokes.setup: Hero about to do something violent
jokes.punchline: Overly apologetic before/during violence
jokes.explanation: Michael's Canadian-level politeness infiltrating his action hero fantasy

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry, I'm really sorry about this.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: This is going to hurt me more than it hurts you.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I apologize in advance.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Please forgive me.

jokes.text: He cut the part where my circuit board malfunctioned? Then what was the point of spilling the drink on me?
jokes.setup: Dwight commenting on editing choices
jokes.punchline: Reveals elaborate robot backstory that was cut
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - taking his robot role seriously with elaborate internal logic

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I had an entire backstory as a robot. They cut it. Fifty-seven pages of robot lore. My processor was a beet.

jokes.text: He finished his movie? No kidding. Wow, that's great. Yeah, good for him.
jokes.setup: Someone telling Packer about Michael's movie
jokes.punchline: Packer's complete disinterest disguised as support
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer - fake enthusiasm masking complete indifference

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Michael made a movie? That's great. I'm sure it's... really something. Look, I gotta get back to work.

jokes.text: Far and away the most expensive shot of the movie, but it was integral to the story.
jokes.setup: Director commentary about expensive shot
jokes.punchline: Treating amateur movie like big-budget production
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusions of grandeur - treating home movie with Hollywood seriousness

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, we spent a lot of money on that shot. I wanted it to look like a big-budget action movie. So I hired a stunt coordinator, rented a camera crane, and we did seventeen takes. Cost us about five thousand dollars. And you know what? It looks exactly like a homemade video made by a bunch of amateurs.

jokes.text: The joke's on you, Goldenface. That man was a wanted animal rapist.
jokes.setup: Hero seemingly upset about innocent death
jokes.punchline: Reveals victim was actually terrible person
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy through shock value - relies on crude reference for humor

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott (as Threat Level Midnight)
dialogue.line: No, no, no. You don't understand. That man... he was a good man. A family man.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott (as Threat Level Midnight)
dialogue.line: But then I realized... he was actually the worst. The absolute worst.

jokes.text: Go puck yourself.
jokes.setup: Action hero delivering one-liner
jokes.punchline: Hockey pun on profanity
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits the hockey setting - economical and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: You're about to get bench-pressed... and I'm not talking about the gym.

jokes.text: That was not scripted.
jokes.setup: Michael breaking character after Jim's explosion
jokes.punchline: Director complaining about actor improvisation
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - reveals Michael's controlling nature as director

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: Cut! Michael, you're breaking character.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Sorry, sorry. Jim just exploded and I couldn't keep a straight face.

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: That's the problem with you actors and your improvisation. Stick to the script!

jokes.text: It'll take a lot more than a bullet to the brain, lungs, heart, back and balls to kill Michael Scarn.
jokes.setup: Hero surviving serious injuries
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously extensive list of fatal wounds survived
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - each body part makes survival more absurd, ending with crude addition

dialogue.speaker: Michael as Scarn
dialogue.line: I've been shot 47 times, stabbed 11 times, set on fire, had my arm ripped off, had it reattached by a street doctor, poisoned, electrocuted, frozen, thawed, burned again, had my face melted off, my face put back on, had my skeleton removed, been hit by a truck, two trucks, a train, and a small bus.

jokes.text: You got problems? My TV don't work. I pay $30 a month for the damn satellite whats-a-whose-it. I can't even get the damn game.
jokes.setup: Hero sharing his serious problems
jokes.punchline: Bartender's mundane TV troubles as counterpoint
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between epic hero problems and everyday complaints

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got some serious problems at home.

dialogue.speaker: Bartender
dialogue.line: Tell me about it. My cable went out for like an hour yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: ...Your cable went out.

dialogue.speaker: Bartender
dialogue.line: I missed the entire first act of Judge Judy.

jokes.text: Ever banged an entire bachelorette party, baby?
jokes.setup: Jan's character trying to be seductive
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately crude pickup line delivered by Jan
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Jan delivering inappropriate line - relies on shock value

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: You know, Michael, I've been thinking about you all day. In fact, I'd like to sit on your face and wiggle around a little bit.

jokes.text: Why are you singling my line out, like, a million years later?
jokes.setup: Jan's embarrassing line being highlighted
jokes.punchline: Jan's defensive reaction to current embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Jan's defensiveness about her cringe moment adds meta layer

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: I'm not a quitter. I'm a doer.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: Don't you dare laugh at me.

jokes.text: The Scarn dance sequence
jokes.setup: Emotional moment of hero regaining confidence
jokes.punchline: Elaborate dance sequence with terrible lyrics
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment - absurd dance with memorable terrible lyrics becomes cultural reference

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know what? I've still got it. Watch this.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm Michael Scott, yes it's true, I'm the best boss in the room, how are you?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I can dance, I can sing, I can do anything, I'm the king of the ring!

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Oh no.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Is he...?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: This is remarkable.

jokes.text: If doing the Scarn is gay, then I'm the biggest queer on earth!
jokes.setup: Hero defending his dance
jokes.punchline: Accidentally self-deprecating logic
jokes.explanation: Michael's poor logic leads to accidental self-own

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: I'm like an action hero, but I dance. I'm... Threat Level Midnight.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's the worst name I've ever heard.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: No, no, no. It's cool because it sounds dangerous, but I'm also graceful.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So you're saying you're dangerous and graceful at the same time?

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Exactly.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's like saying you're a brilliant idiot.

jokes.text: I think I was just relieved to see that Michael Scarn got his confidence back.
jokes.setup: Jim apologizing for laughing
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically treating movie character as real person
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim - fake sincerity that's actually mocking the movie's logic

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I'm sorry. It's just... you're upset that a fictional character didn't call you back?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Should we send flowers to the movie theater? Maybe leave a strongly worded letter at the box office?

jokes.text: You should enter it in festivals. Or carnivals.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to enter movie in festivals
jokes.punchline: Addition of 'carnivals' undermines the compliment
jokes.explanation: Pam's gentle cruelty - seeming supportive while actually insulting

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You should submit your movie to some film festivals.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Really? You think it's good enough?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, or carnivals.

jokes.text: I loved that you got to work together with all your friends. Isn't that so great when you can all work together like that?
jokes.setup: Holly asked what she loved about the movie
jokes.punchline: Completely avoids discussing movie quality, focuses on process
jokes.explanation: Holly's diplomatic non-answer reveals her true opinion while trying to be kind

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: I loved how they really captured the essence of the story, you know? And the cinematography - the way they framed each shot was just so intentional. Plus, the production design, the costumes, everything came together so seamlessly.

jokes.text: This isn't Ocean's Eleven, where you get together with all your friends, and you just have fun, and you don't care about how it turns out.
jokes.setup: Michael rejecting Holly's friendship-focused compliment
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describes exactly what his movie is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - rejects accurate description while missing the irony

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's not a compliment, Holly. That's like saying, 'Hey, you'd be great at friendship.' Well, yeah. I'm great at a lot of things. I'm great at business. I'm great at drinking beer. I'm great at volleyball.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Michael...

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I want to be great at something that matters. Like my movie. It's about a guy who's really good at friendship and uses that to solve crimes.

jokes.text: I have my book on business, Somehow I Manage. I have my HBO comedy special, 'Here I Go Again...'
jokes.setup: Michael listing his other creative projects
jokes.punchline: References to long-running fake projects
jokes.explanation: Callback to Michael's various delusional projects throughout the series

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I've been working on a screenplay for like, eight years now. It's called 'Threat Level Midnight.' I've also been developing a novel, a children's book, and I'm pretty sure I invented the phrase 'that's what she said' before anyone else.

jokes.text: I am a huge Woody Allen fan. Although I've only seen Antz.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming to be a huge fan
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only seen one movie, and it's not even a Woody Allen film
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character comedy - perfectly captures Michael's false expertise and pop culture confusion

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm a huge Woody Allen fan.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Really? Which of his films is your favorite?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh, well I've only seen one movie, actually.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Which one?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Antz.

jokes.text: I thought A Bug's Life was better, much better than Antz. Point is, don't listen to your critics. Listen to your fans.
jokes.setup: Michael defending Woody Allen's integrity
jokes.punchline: Admits he preferred the competing movie, then draws wrong lesson
jokes.explanation: Great Michael logic - contradicts his own point then draws self-serving conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Woody Allen is a great filmmaker. His movies are works of art.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I mean, I preferred Match Point, but that doesn't mean anything.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That was directed by Christopher Nolan, so... Woody Allen is still a genius.

jokes.text: We filmed this during an actual Scranton High School hockey game. They were trying to qualify for states... They were disqualified. They had to forfeit the game. Undefeated season.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining filming logistics
jokes.punchline: Escalating revelation of how badly he ruined their season
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - each detail makes Michael's disruption worse, delivered with complete obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So we're filming this season in the office, but I realized we need to move some things around for the cameras.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I may have accidentally knocked over the water cooler.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And the filing cabinets. All of them.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I also rearranged everyone's desks into a circle because I thought it would be more intimate for the documentary.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And I kind of... set a small fire in the break room. But it was a very small fire.

jokes.text: I worked in a gold factory. We had a boss who only cared about money.
jokes.setup: Villain explaining his tragic backstory
jokes.punchline: Boss at gold factory caring about money is completely normal
jokes.explanation: Smart joke - villain's 'tragic' backstory is actually completely logical business practice

dialogue.speaker: Jim as Goldenface
dialogue.line: My father ran a gold factory. But he only cared about one thing: money. So I became Goldenface.

jokes.text: Hey, we got sports games again.
jokes.setup: Earlier complaint about satellite TV not working
jokes.punchline: TV suddenly working during climactic moment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier TV problems, resolved at convenient moment

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So the satellite TV just magically started working?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's not magic, it's superior timing and technical expertise.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Or it just needed to be turned off and back on again.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's what I said.

jokes.text: Yeah, I guess I did let him be a robot.
jokes.setup: Dwight serving breakfast like a robot butler
jokes.punchline: Jim's grudging admission about Dwight's robot performance
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Jim finally acknowledges Dwight's robot-like behavior

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I have to admit, that was the most convincing robot butler performance I've ever seen.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Which is saying something, because I've seen a lot of robot butlers.

jokes.text: Hey, whoa, whoa, whoa, isn't the President evil? Oh, yeah. Yes, he is.
jokes.setup: Hero accepting mission from the President
jokes.punchline: Suddenly remembering the President is the villain
jokes.explanation: Good plot hole humor - characters suddenly realize basic story problem

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I accept this mission, Mr. President.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait... you're the villain!

jokes.text: No, no, he's doing it to catch the President. No, no, Dwight, he's just being stupid.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to rationalize plot inconsistency
jokes.punchline: Jim dismissing it as poor writing
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about audience trying to make sense of bad movie logic

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well, maybe the character just forgot about that earlier episode.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Or maybe it's just bad writing.

jokes.text: Well, because I am Michael Scarn.
jokes.setup: Narrator revealing why he knows so much about Michael Scarn
jokes.punchline: Michael as narrator revealing he IS the character
jokes.explanation: Standard twist ending - predictable but fits Michael's ego-driven storytelling

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You might be wondering how I know so much about Michael Scarn. Well, that's because I am Michael Scarn.

tmdb_overview: Traveling salesman Todd Packer comes to Dunder Mifflin looking for a desk job in the office. However, the office is unsure if they want him to work there due to his previous behavior. After dealing with computer problems, Andy confronts office administrator Pam to get a new computer.
insight: Todd Packer episode trades setup for character chaos, landing 43 jokes in brisk pace.
